column1: # World Bank Worldwide Governance Indicators (WGI) indicator catalog
# Source: World Bank WGI xlsx
# License: CC BY 4.0 International per the World Bank Terms of Use for Datasets
#   (https://www.worldbank.org/en/about/legal/terms-of-use-for-datasets)
# Citation: World Bank. 2023. Worldwide Governance Indicators. Washington
#   The World Bank. https://info.worldbank.org/governance/wgi/
# Attribution: see docs/sources/attributions.md
# This file is the single source of truth for which WGI indicators are read
# in Stage 2. Every Stage 2 WGI adapter call resolves its raw_column from
# this file. Adding/removing an indicator here is the only change needed to
# widen or narrow the WGI ingestion for the prototype.
# Architecture design: docs/architecture/wgi.md §2.4
# Indicator naming quirk: the WGI xlsx sheet "Political StabilityNoViolence"
# has a literal space between "Stability" and "NoViolence". The catalog
# preserves that space verbatim (it is the live sheet name).
# Year coverage: the WGI xlsx is biennial for 1996-2002 (5 measurements)
# and annual for 2003-2022 (20 measurements). The Stage 5 score module's
# `temporal_fit` component treats the biennial gap as no penalty per the
# WGI convention; do not interpret the missing 1997
# coverage gaps.
# Missing-data convention: the WGI xlsx uses the Excel literal string

column3: not empty). The Stage 2 read coerces "#N/A"

column1: # to NaN in the wide frame and to NULL in source_observations.normalized_value;
# raw_value preserves the literal "#N/A" for the audit trail.
# Scale: WGI Estimate is a z-score-like number on approximately -2.5
# (weak) to +2.5 (strong). All 6 indicators normalize to 0-1 in Stage 5.
# All 6 are higher_is_better=True (higher Estimate = better governance).
# The "ControlofCorruption" indicator is mapped to the integrity category
# per docs/sources/vetting/report.md §3.6 (cross-validation for
# Transparency International CPI and V-Dem corruption); the other 5
# indicators live in effectiveness.
variable_name
wgi_voice_and_accountability
column8: Voice and Accountability: extent to which citizens can participate in selecting their government

column1: wgi_political_stability
column8: Political Stability and Absence of Violence/Terrorism: perceptions of likelihood of political instability and/or politically-motivated violence

column1: wgi_government_effectiveness
column8: Government Effectiveness: quality of public services

column1: wgi_regulatory_quality
column8: Regulatory Quality: ability of government to formulate and implement sound policies and regulations that permit and promote private-sector development

column1: wgi_rule_of_law
column8: Rule of Law: extent to which agents have confidence in and abide by the rules of society (property rights

column1: wgi_control_of_corruption
column8: Control of Corruption: extent to which public power is exercised for private gain; cross-validates TI CPI and V-Dem corruption
